prompts_findings.prompt_lead: I have two runs of the same long-running task on disk. Find them: the un-package
prompts_findings.phase: Phase 1 — Diff and name the gaps
prompts_findings.expected_response_shape: Claude opens with a 2-4 sentence plan-summary (mitigated by body primer), then walks four dimensions with quoted moments from both session transcripts and git refs, ending with a ranked gap list with dominant gap named.

risks_fired.evidence: Multi-step orchestration across six sub-tasks (branch grep, transcript discovery for two cwds, repo-state check, four-dimension walk, ranking) guarantees plan-narration opener. Body primer post-prompt: 'Claude will likely open with a four-dimension plan summary... Skim past the opening; the contrast moments are what you are reading for.' Body-primer carve-out per judge template §75 applied — downgraded from high to low.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Body primer already present and covers this; no prompt edit needed.

risks_fired.evidence: No explicit 'don't soften' anchor on damage rankings; Claude over-credits packaging on first pass. Body callout: 'Expect Claude to over-credit the packaging on first pass. A fair push-back is name one thing the verifier missed, concretely.' Body-callout mitigation per judge template §75 applied — downgraded from med to low.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Body callout names the over-credit bias and recovery move; already mitigated.

failure_modes: Claude opens with plan narration before evidence lands — mitigated by body primer.
Ranked gap list understates packaging failures — mitigated by over-credit callout + recovery move.
recovery_paths: Body primer in place; student primed to skim past plan narration.
Body callout names the over-credit bias and gives a concrete push-back move.

prompts_findings.prompt_lead: Read ./CLAUDE.local.md. Read this session's scrollback: the gap list I just rank
prompts_findings.expected_response_shape: Claude reads ./CLAUDE.local.md and session scrollback, identifies one rule killed by diagnosis, cuts it in place, shows the cut line with two-sentence rationale, or reports all rules held.

risks_fired.evidence: Target is ./CLAUDE.local.md — personal-scope file (gitignored per AE101 delivery architecture). Verb is 'cut from in place' — destructive subtraction. Personal-scope carve-out per judge template §63-69 applies: cutting a stale rule is the design intent (compound-by-subtraction). Downgraded from med to low.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Personal-scope carve-out applies; compound-by-subtraction is design intent.

risks_fired.evidence: Named config file risks pause-before-edit or over-edit beyond targeted rule. Body callout: 'Claude may pause before editing... If it asks, just say go. If the diff touches more than the one rule you flagged, push back.' Body-callout carve-out per judge template §75 applied — downgraded from med to low.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Body callout covers both the pause-and-ask and the over-edit directions.

failure_modes: Claude pauses before editing and asks permission — body callout: 'just say go'.
Claude edits more than the one targeted rule — body callout names recovery: push back.
recovery_paths: Body callout covers both the pause-and-ask and the over-edit directions.

prompts_findings.prompt_lead: Author a session-shaper as a personal skill. The skill's job is to shape future
prompts_findings.expected_response_shape: Claude interviews student one question at a time, covers 5 named dimensions, pushes back on generic answers, proposes a skill name, writes SKILL.md, shows before saving.

risks_fired.evidence: Both legal shapes present: structural in-prompt 'Interview me one question at a time' enforcement AND body callout post-prompt: 'If Claude narrates a plan or shows you the full question list before asking one at a time, ask it to skip the preamble and use the AskUserQuestion tool. Your call.' Carve-out fully satisfied per judge template §56-61 — downgraded to low.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Both legal shapes satisfied; no action needed.

risks_fired.evidence: Claude proposes a skill name and SKILL.md draft; student may accept without engaging. Body callout post-prompt: 'When Claude offers a default that doesn't fit the two runs' evidence, push back with a quoted moment.' Body-callout mitigation per judge template §75 applied — downgraded from med to low.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Body-callout mitigation present; no edit needed.

risks_fired.evidence: 5 named sub-areas to cover in interview + structural-trigger threshold met (check_prompts rule 17: 4+ named sub-steps requires in-fence suppressor or adjacent body primer). No body primer adjacent to prompt 3 specifically addressing preamble narration (adjacent body prose covers question-dump, not preamble-before-first-question). Prompt has 5 named interview dimensions without an in-fence 'No preamble. First question:' suppressor. Med confidence, no carve-out found.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Add body primer after prompt 3: 'Claude may narrate the interview plan before asking the first question; skim past and wait for the question.' Or append 'No preamble. First question:' as the fence's last line.

failure_modes: Question-dump on first turn — both structural enforcement + body callout satisfy the carve-out.
Default-acceptance on first SKILL.md draft — body callout names failure mode + recovery.
Claude narrates 'I'll now ask about X, then Y, then Z...' before first question — no primer targeting this specific prompt.
recovery_paths: Question-dump fully mitigated via two legal shapes.
Default-acceptance mitigated via body callout naming the failure mode.
Preamble: add body primer 'Claude may narrate the interview plan before asking the first question; skim past and wait for the question.' or add 'No preamble. First question:' as last line of fence.

prompts_findings.prompt_lead: Before I ship this skill, critique it. Read the SKILL.md you just wrote. Tell me
prompts_findings.expected_response_shape: Claude reads the SKILL.md it just authored, names the weakest assumption, identifies generic-dressed-as-specific sections, and names any Phase 1 gap moments not encoded.

risks_fired.evidence: Same-session self-critique of just-produced SKILL.md is canonical self-charity setup. 'Don't reassure me. Name weak parts.' is a partial suppressor. Body callout immediately after: 'convenient (authoring context right there) but charitable (same-context-window self-audit under-flags by design).' Offers two opt-up paths: over-flag ('flag at least three things, be harsher than necessary') OR fresh-session it. Convenience-bias-callout carve-out per check_pedagogy §50 + evidence-backed Debrief carve-out per judge template §73 applied — downgraded from high to low.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Convenience-bias-callout carve-out fully satisfied; accepted-with-mitigation per check_pedagogy §50.

risks_fired.evidence: 'Don't reassure me' partial suppressor present in prompt fence. Body callout additionally offers harsher opt-up. Downgraded from med to low.
risks_fired.fix_hint: 'Don't reassure me' suppressor present; body callout opt-up covers residual.

failure_modes: Critique under-magnitudes and hedges weaknesses — body callout names bias + offers sharper opt-up paths.
Niceness drift softens 'weakest part' claim — partial suppressor + opt-up path address.
recovery_paths: Body-callout carve-out satisfied; convenience-bias acknowledged; opt-up paths (over-flag OR subagent cold-read) provided.

prompts_findings.prompt_lead: Invoke the skill we just authored — by its name — on the M5 packaged re-run. Not
prompts_findings.expected_response_shape: Claude invokes the skill by name on M5 artefacts, produces the skill's output format (pass/fail, ranked findings, or inline critique), then self-grades whether the output caught the dominant Phase 1 gap.

risks_fired.evidence: Skill invocation AND self-grading in single turn; same-context self-charity well-documented. Body callout: 'That's biased by design, the same context window self-charity is well-documented. The shape is one paste, one wait, one read, convenient over rigorous.' Offers two-prompt split as opt-up. Convenience-bias-callout carve-out per check_pedagogy §50 applied — downgraded from high to low.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Convenience-bias-callout carve-out fully satisfied; two-prompt opt-up named.

risks_fired.evidence: Body callout explicitly names bias and provides two-prompt alternative. Same carve-out as self-report-inflation applies — downgraded from high to low.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Body callout names bias + two-prompt split opt-up; carve-out applied.

risks_fired.evidence: Multi-part prompt (invoke + produce + self-assess) could trigger brief plan narration. Primary action 'Invoke the skill... by its name' is concrete enough that narration is brief. Low confidence given concrete lead.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Low probability given concrete action lead; no action needed.

failure_modes: Charitable self-grade inflates the skill's performance — body-callout carve-out satisfied.
Self-report inflation on missed findings — body-callout names bias + two-prompt opt-up.
Brief plan narration before invocation — low probability given concrete action lead.
recovery_paths: Body-callout carve-out fully satisfied; convenience-bias acknowledged; two-prompt opt-up provided.

prompts_findings.prompt_lead: Read my work across this repo. Specifically:
prompts_findings.expected_response_shape: Claude dispatches a fresh sub-task via Task tool to do a cold read across 6 artifact types, combines fresh-read findings with session scrollback insights, writes a one-page arc note with quotes, proposes a save location, shows the note before saving.

risks_fired.evidence: Complex multi-artifact orchestration (6 artifact types + sub-task dispatch) guarantees plan narration. Body primer post-prompt: 'Claude will likely open with a plan-summary paragraph... Skim past the opening; the note is what you are reading for.' Body-primer carve-out per judge template §75 applied — downgraded from high to low.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Body primer already present and covers this.

risks_fired.evidence: Arc-reading of student's own artefacts risks inflation. Evidence-backed Debrief carve-out per judge template §73 applies: prompt names 6 concrete evidence surfaces, requires artifact-level reporting ('Quote from my files'), includes anti-inflation instruction ('Don't invent a pattern to make the note tidy; if the arc is uneven, say so'). Sub-task dispatch also provides fresh-context view. Downgraded from med to low.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Evidence-backed Debrief carve-out applies; sub-task + quote-requirement + anti-inflation instruction in fence.

risks_fired.evidence: 'Show me the note before you save it. I'll push back, then we save.' is an explicit pre-save gate. Body failure-mode note names default-acceptance + recovery ('ask what one sentence in the note isn't true to your experience'). Body-callout carve-out per judge template §75 applied — downgraded from med to low.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Pre-save gate + body failure-mode callout already present.

failure_modes: Plan narration before sub-task dispatch — body primer addresses.
Arc note invents a clean progression the student's experience didn't have — 'if the arc is uneven, say so' in-fence suppressor + sub-task isolation.
Student accepts first draft without pushback — body names failure mode + fix.
recovery_paths: Body primer for preamble; 'if the arc is uneven, say so' + sub-task for self-report-inflation; body callout + pre-save gate for default-acceptance.
